metadata.title: Q&A on the revision of the guidelines on clinical investigation of recombinant and human plasma-derived factor VIII products (EMA/CHMP/BPWP/144533/2009 Rev. 2) and FIX products (EMA/CHMP/BPWP/144552/2009 Rev. 2)
metadata.notes: Short 2-page Q&A clarifying the transition from mandatory PUP clinical trials to registry-based post-authorisation studies for FVIII and FIX products.

rules.rule_text: For new recombinant and human plasma-derived factor VIII and FIX product developments, the obligation to conduct a clinical trial in Previously Untreated Patients (PUPs) is removed. PUP data must instead be provided through post-authorisation measures based on haemophilia registry data.
rules.source_section: How this will impact the marketing authorisation?
rules.verbatim_quote: For new recombinant and human plasma-derived factor product developments, the obligation in the PIP to conduct a clinical trial in PUPs will no longer be required; this will be replaced by the provision of PUPs data coming from post-authorisation measure based on registries' data.
rules.exceptions: Applies only under Rev. 2 of the FVIII and FIX guidelines. Products with ongoing PUP trials have different requirements (see EMA-FVIII-QA-002).

rules.rule_text: For FVIII/FIX products where a clinical trial in PUPs is ongoing or completed, the results of that study must be provided in PSUR/PSUSA submissions. Additionally, registry-based PUP data from post-authorisation measures is also required.
rules.source_section: How this will impact the marketing authorisation?
rules.verbatim_quote: For products where a clinical trial in PUPs is ongoing (in the case when interim results are available) or completed, the results of the study should be provided in PSUR/PSUSA submissions. Provision of PUPs data coming from post-authorisation measure based on registries' data will also be required.
rules.exceptions: Only applies to products that already have ongoing or completed PUP clinical trials.

rules.rule_text: MAHs with agreed Paediatric Investigation Plans (PIPs) that include PUP clinical trials must submit a PIP modification to the PDCO to remove the PUP study requirement, rather than unilaterally discontinuing the obligation.
rules.source_section: How will this impact the Paediatric Investigation Plan?
rules.verbatim_quote: For any changes requested for an agreed PIP, the MAHs would need to submit a PIP modification to the PDCO in order to propose changes e.g. with respect to removing a requirement to conduct a PUP study.
rules.exceptions: Only relevant for products with existing agreed PIPs that include PUP study obligations.

rules.rule_text: Post-authorisation PUP safety data for FVIII and FIX products must be collected through existing haemophilia registries using a defined set of core data elements, rather than through dedicated clinical trials.
rules.verbatim_quote: the aim of the current revision was to remove the obligation to perform clinical trials in previously untreated patients and instead to request post-authorisation studies based on a set of core data elements to be collected in haemophilia registries.

rules.rule_text: Inhibitor development monitoring remains a critical safety concern for FVIII products in PUPs, particularly during the first fifty exposure days. The shift to registry-based monitoring does not diminish the requirement to collect and report inhibitor development data.
rules.verbatim_quote: Inhibitor development, associated with a reduction in efficacy of the haemostatic effect of Factor VIII and occurring very commonly during the first fifty days of treatment with Factor VIII products in Previously Untreated Patients (PUPs), is one of the most serious complications in the management of severe haemophilia A.
rules.exceptions: To a lesser degree, also applies to Factor IX in haemophilia B.
